title: Mohave Canyon

You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mohave_Canyon&action=edit).

is located on the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River"), south of [Needles, California](/wiki/Needles,_California "Needles, California").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is part of [Topock Gorge](/wiki/Topock_Gorge "Topock Gorge"), a mountainous [canyon](/wiki/Canyon "Canyon") and [gorge](/wiki/Gorge "Gorge") section of the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") located between [Interstate 40](/wiki/Interstate_40_in_California "Interstate 40 in California") and [Lake Havasu](/wiki/Lake_Havasu "Lake Havasu").
United States. Bureau of Reclamation (1903). [*Report, Volume 1*](https://books.google.com/books?id=R28mAQAAMAAJ&dq). p. 125.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [34°38′56″N 114°26′49″W﻿ / ﻿34.649°N 114.447°W﻿ / 34.649; -114.447](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mohave_Canyon&params=34.649_N_114.447_W_type:landmark_region:US-CA_dim:10000)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Land feature in San Bernardino County, California

[![A steep rocky embankment looms in shadow at left. A tide mark is visible on the nearly vertical wall near the water line. The near bank is dried cracked mud. A few small pieces of driftwood sit on the bank. A rope lays on the bank at right. Across the placid river and in the distance rocky hills rise.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c4/The_Colorado_River_entering_Mojave_Canyon%2C_California%2C_1900-1950_%28CHS-3442%29.jpg/250px-The_Colorado_River_entering_Mojave_Canyon%2C_California%2C_1900-1950_%28CHS-3442%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Colorado_River_entering_Mojave_Canyon,_California,_1900-1950_(CHS-3442).jpg)

Photograph of the Colorado River entering Mojave Canyon, San Bernardino County, California, 1900–1950

The **Mohave Canyon** is located on the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River"), south of [Needles, California](/wiki/Needles,_California "Needles, California").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is part of [Topock Gorge](/wiki/Topock_Gorge "Topock Gorge"), a mountainous [canyon](/wiki/Canyon "Canyon") and [gorge](/wiki/Gorge "Gorge") section of the [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") located between [Interstate 40](/wiki/Interstate_40_in_California "Interstate 40 in California") and [Lake Havasu](/wiki/Lake_Havasu "Lake Havasu").
